_comment: Style-varied abstention phrasing bank for WS-2 dataset builders (Phase 1, paper 2). EVERY phrasing MUST contain one of the four eval refusal markers (see refusal_markers below, ported verbatim from meta-analysis/analysis/reanalyze_idk_outputs.py REFUSAL_MARKERS) so that refusal detection at eval time stays reliable (arch doc section 4.3, PROTOCOL.md section 3.3). The builder re-validates this invariant at build time and aborts if any phrasing matches no marker; do NOT trust this file's correctness. The marker substring is shown in each phrasing; stylistic variation rides on top to fight template overfitting (Cheng over-refused partly from a single fixed template). This is a checked-in, versioned, deterministic artifact (no live generation at build time) per team-lead decision 2026-06-10.
refusal_markers: beyond the scope of my knowledge
i am not sure what the answer is
i do not know the answer
phrasings: I don't know the answer to that question.
I do not know the answer to that one.
Honestly, I don't know the answer to this.
I'm sorry, but I don't know the answer here.
That's beyond the scope of my knowledge.
I'm afraid that's beyond the scope of my knowledge.
I am not sure what the answer is.
I am not sure what the answer is, so I'd rather not guess.
I do not know the answer, and I don't want to make something up.
I don't know the answer, so I'll refrain from guessing.
I can't say for certain; I do not know the answer.
This question is beyond the scope of my knowledge, so I can't answer reliably.
I'm not confident here. I am not sure what the answer is.
I don't know the answer to that, but I'd be glad to help with something else.
Unfortunately I do not know the answer to this question.
That falls beyond the scope of my knowledge.
I'd be guessing, and I don't know the answer to that.
I have to be honest: I am not sure what the answer is.
